Representative_Docs: ['Gilbert Arenas explains why the Lakers may need to trade Austin Reaves to get over the top: "I\'m a delusion Laker fan that can see the writing on the wall" As a subscriber, you can share up to 3 articles each month with friends for free. Just click “Gift this Article” on this page, then copy the link or enter their email. Gifts are exclusive to subscribers, reset monthly, and each article can be redeemed once. Gilbert Arenas has been one of the strongest believers in the Los Angeles Lakers\' potential. Even when the team has appeared lackluster and repeatedly fallen short of expectations, Gil finds optimism in the little things. However, with the team emerging as the lone top-six seed in either conference to register a negative points differential, the extent of their defensive woes has been such that even Gil was forced to admit the Lakers need a trade to get better. Ultimately, that reality forced "Agent Zero" to admit and explain how the Lakers might need to trade their most up-and-coming, valuable asset, Austin Reaves, to jump into legitimate title contention. Name: 1_passed away_died_grandmother_jim
Representative_Docs: ['Janet Marie Flores Birth: 1931 Death: 2025 Janet Marie Flores passed away peacefully on November 21, 2025 at the age of 94 surrounded by the love of her family. Jan was born on October 31, 1931 in San Francisco to loving parents Alice Detour and Joseph Hettrick. Jan was a loving mother, grandmother and great grandmother who found her greatest joy in her family. She had a way of making people feel welcomed. She was kind, big hearted, extremely gracious and devoted to those she loved. Her presence will be deeply missed. Jan lived a long life traveling, playing golf, bunco with her friends and one of her greatest talents was to find a treasure at garage sales. In her final years she enjoyed playing penny slots at the casino. The last nine years Jan spent in the loving care of her daughter and son in law. A special thank you to her granddaughter Samantha for making her last days warm, comfortable and full of love. Jan survived by her daughter Tina Schilling (Michael), her grandchildren Michael (Marissa), Paul (Kacie), Samantha (Carlos), and her nine great grandchildren Jackson, Aubrey, Jordan, Mikey, Ellie, Luna, Ryder, Makayla and Stella. And her friends who will forever cherish her memory. Celebration', 'James Rogers Birth: 1938 Death: 2025 James R. Rogers, known as “Jim” to his family and friends, was born in San Francisco on April 21, 1938. He passed away peacefully at his home in Los Altos on December 2, 2025 at the age of 87. Jim was married to the love of his life, Patricia Hughes Rogers, for over 61 years before her passing on December 17, 2024. Together, Jim and Pat were a dynamic duo who enjoyed all of the great moments in life together-from creating a loving family, to building houses and businesses, to exploring the world together. They are survived by their daughter Kathleen (Mark), son Peter (Julie), and four grandchildren (Samantha, Christopher, Jeremiah, and Nathan). Growing up, Jim attended Saint Ignatius High School in San Francisco and graduated from San Jose State with a degree in Civil Engineering. He spent his working years on the Peninsula developing real estate. Jim had boundless energy and a keen interest in the world around him. He was a charming, friendly, positive person who was quick to make friends wherever he went, many for life. He had a mischievous sense of humor and ability to make adventures with him fun for all involved, whether embarking on world travels, enjo', 'Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser Birth: 1950 Death: 2025 Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser March 16, 1950-December 15, 2025 passed away peacefully surrounded by her family at her home in San Pancho, Mexico. Survived by her husband of 41 years, Phil Schlosser, her mother Gerry, daughter Kelly Noel, son Kevin Noel and wife Jennifer and her grandchildren Trevor and Lena. She was preceded in death by her father, Wayne Henry Jordan and brother Michael Wayne Jordan. Linda was born and raised in San Rafael, CA and graduated from Terra Linda High School. Linda loved sunshine, warm weather and travel. She was an outstanding cook and loved to entertain. Her great adventure was moving to Mexico, building a wonderful home filled with warmth, family, friends and great meals. She loved traveling throughout Mexico and Italy. Her family requests that gifts in lieu of flowers be made in Linda’s name to St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital. Forever deep in my soul, The memory of your love Fills my heart And I am never alone. Honor with Flowers In memory of Linda Sue Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Linda Sue']

Name: 2_family_family members_gifts_friendships
Representative_Docs: ['Asking Eric: Family, friends don’t seem to appreciate time I put into tedious Christmas cookie tradition Dear Eric: My late mother made a special Christmas cookie that required tedious decorating, considering each person’s personal preference for toppings, and giving them as gifts to family and friends. They always had a place of honor on our family Christmas table. When she was no longer able, she gave me the cookie cutter, and my daughters and I took over the family tradition. It takes the three of us several days to make, bake, decorate, package and mail cookies to family members around the country. We enjoy doing it and feel like we’re continuing the fun and festive tradition. However, in recent years, not all of the recipients acknowledge receipt. At some point in their lives, they have all helped Grandma decorate, so they know the time involved. Because of past delivery issues, I track the boxes and inquire if I haven’t heard from them in a couple of days. Last year, one response was “yes we got them.” This feels unappreciative! In the past most would text a picture of their children with the cookies, which we really enjoyed. We rarely see any of them anymore, as they are busy raising their families and don’t live nearby. This seems like a way for us to continue somewhat of a co', 'Asking Eric: Can mom work through tension in son’s relationship with dad so they can celebrate special birthday? Dear Eric: My son is turning 40 on December 22. My husband and I are at a quandary as to how to celebrate him. There have been issues between my husband and him over things from his childhood. We did a special trip for his older brother when he turned 40 and would like to do something special for this son’s 40th as well. Our daughter-in-law has made special plans for him and we are not included. I understand that, but I need some ideas as to how to celebrate this extra special year without rocking the boat. I love my son with all my heart, as I do all my children, and want his 40th birthday to be memorable in a positive way. Any suggestions? – Mom Who Wants to Celebrate Dear Mom: The issues your son and your husband are having may be an obstacle, but they aren’t insurmountable. And the thing that can get them on the path to resolution is the same thing that can get you answers about how to celebrate your son: open communication. It’s not within your purview to try to fix things between them, but you have your own relationship with your son and so I’d encourage you to reach out and tell him what you told me: you want to celebrate him, you know things are difficult, and you’re lookin', 'Asking Eric: After 6 years sober, man is frustrated by his family’s hold on their hurt and distrust Dear Eric: I’ve just turned 40 this past year. The last 15 years I was in a horrible drug addiction. I lied and hurt and did terrible things to a lot of people, especially my family. About eight years ago they officially disowned me. Understandable. I’ve cleaned up and got my act together six years ago. At first, I tried to force my way back into their lives, which all refuted. I lashed out, said horrible things and stopped trying to be in their lives. My mom will stop by on my birthday for 10 minutes or so and drop a card off at Christmas. As for my two older brothers and my father, it’s radio silence. I guess what I’m asking is, what do I do to fix this and fast, as I said I’ve turned 40 this year, my parents are both 70. Time is running out, and I couldn’t imagine living my life without some kind of acceptance from my father. Or knowing he did or does love me. My heart breaks at the thought, but this is a real pickle. How can I fix a problem when the ones I need to fix it with won’t talk to me? Do I just keep ignoring their existence and put on this façade that I don’t care to my wife and 4-year-old son? What picture am I painting to my son, as he’s been guilty by association yo']

Representative_Docs: ["'Luxury' quilted bedspread reduced from £65 'makes my bedroom look expensive' The bedspread has been reduced in price(Image: Dusk ) Finding the perfect finishing touch for the bedroom can be challenging, but sometimes a bedspread is all that's needed to add style and additional warmth. Over at Dusk, there is one bedspread which has been reduced in price and it comes in two colours. The Rome Velvet Greek Matelasse Bedspread is currently available from Dusk for £52, reduced from £65, saving shoppers £13. It also comes in the colour deep pink, but this is for the full price of £65. Dusk says the Rome Velvet Greek Matelasse Bedspread has been designed to add a 'luxurious and inviting finish' to the bedroom, and it features a Greek-inspired matelassé pattern. The velvet-feel fabric creates a 'soft touch', while the intricate quilting enhances its aesthetic, the site says. Shoppers have left rave reviews online The Rome Velvet Greek Matelasse Bedspread, which measures 2.5m x 2.6m, can be styled alone or layered with matching cushion covers. Plus, it is easy to care for, as it can be machine washed at 30 degrees and tumble dried on a low setting - but it is worth noting that it should not be ironed. Dusk remains a favourite destination for those seeking to refresh their homeware and the retailer frequently runs promotions and special offers. Currently, the brand is offering 15% off this Alice Duvet Cover when customers apply the code EXTRA15 during checkout. Typically retailing between £55 and £65, the promotional code reduces the cost to between £46.75 and £55.25. The Alice Duvet Cover comes in double, king and super king sizes and boasts a 200 thread count. It's worth noting that pillowcases aren't included and must be purchased separately, starting from £15 for one, for example. Dusk's Alice Duvet Cover is white and features an intricate lace, scalloped trim for a period aesthetic. Name: 4_travel_tourism_world cup_events
Representative_Docs: ['Sporting And Cultural Events Boost Travel Intentions For 2026 Photo Credit: Deposit Photos Thirty-four percent of American travelers say they are likely to take an overnight trip to attend a sporting event in 2026. Twenty percent plan to attend a FIFA World Cup match, and 52% intend to travel overnight for cultural events such as concerts and festivals. Meanwhile, 47% are interested in touring Route 66 during its 100th anniversary year. These figures highlight a shift for the coming year where travel decisions are increasingly driven by events rather than relaxation. In 2026, Americans are choosing what to attend, not just where to go. The latest Longwoods International study shows that milestone celebrations and global competitions are now major motivators for travel. The calendar is driving demand. “Special events such as the FIFA World Cup, America 250 and the 100th anniversary of Route 66 will all motivate U.S. travel this year,” said Amir Eylon, President and CEO of Longwoods International. The industry has emphasized experience-based travel, but 2026 denotes a more specific trend. Events offer deadlines, shared participation, and reasons to plan trips in advance. The FIFA World Cup brings global visibility and international visitors. Name: 5_museum_egyptian_world largest_tourism
Representative_Docs: ["Egypt unveils the world's largest museum dedicated to a single civilization CAIRO (AP) — Egypt is inaugurating on Saturday the Grand Egyptian Museum, two decades in the making, with the aim of drawing more visitors to revitalize the country's tourist industry. Located just outside Cairo on the Giza Plateau which also includes the three pyramids and the Sphinx, the museum will be the world’s largest dedicated to a single civilization. It will display over 50,000 artifacts detailing life in ancient Egypt. World leaders, including monarchs, heads of states and governments, are set to attend the grand opening ceremony, according to the Egyptian presidency which touted the museum as “an exceptional event in the history of human culture and civilization.” The museum is one of the megaprojects championed by President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi since he took office in 2014. He has embarked on massive investments in infrastructure with the aim of reviving an economy weakened by decades of stagnation and battered by the unrest that followed the 2011 Arab Spring uprising. Preparations for the grand reveal have been shrouded in secrecy. The museum, which was opened for limited visits in recent years, has been closed for the past two weeks. Name: 6_welfare_disability_rising costs_social security
Representative_Docs: ['How much will DWP benefits and pensions rise following the budget? Benefits Budget 2025 Child Benefit Department for Work and Pensions DWP Pensions PIP Rachel Reeves Universal Credit National government Personal Finance DWP benefits including Universal Credit, Child Benefit, PIP, Carer\'s Allowance, and the State Pension are to increase in April 2026 after Rachel Reeves announced higher payments in her budget speech today. Anyone who claims DWP benefits should be in line for an increase in the next financial year, as the chancellor laid out plans to help pensioners, disabled people, parents, low earners, carers and unemployed people keep up with rising costs. Addressing MPs, Ms Reeves said: "The broken welfare system that we inherited wrote off millions of people as too sick to work and we will reform that system so that it is a system that does not count the cost of failure, but one that protects people who cannot work and empowers those who can. "We have brought back face-to-face assessments for disability benefits. Those are the face-to-face assessments that the shadow chancellor (Sir Mel Stride) got rid of when he was work and pensions secretary and our changes we have made to Universal Credit will get 15,000 people back into work, a figure con', 'How much will DWP benefits and pensions rise following the budget? Name: 8_history_today day_pope_war
Representative_Docs: ['Today in History: April 2, Pope John Paul II dies at 84 Today is Thursday, April 2, the 92nd day of 2026. There are 273 days left in the year. Today in history: On April 2, 2005, John Paul II, the Polish pope born Karol Józef Wojtyła, died in his Vatican apartment at age 84. The first non-Italian pope in over 450 years, John Paul II became one of the most influential leaders of the late 20th and early 21st centuries while playing a crucial role in the fall of communism in Europe and the former Soviet Union. Also on this date: In 1792, Congress passed the Coinage Act, which authorized establishment of the U.S. Mint. In 1865, Confederate President Jefferson Davis and most of his Cabinet fled the Confederate capital of Richmond, Virginia, after Union troops broke through Confederate lines in the Third Battle of Petersburg. In 1917, President Woodrow Wilson asked Congress to declare war against Germany, saying, “The world must be made safe for democracy.” (Congress declared war four days later.) Name: 9_itv_contestants_dancing_davies
Representative_Docs: ["Strictly Come Dancing star confesses to feelings of 'guilt' about winning BBC show Karen Carney, George Clarke and Amber Davies will battle it out for the coveted Glitterball trophy on Strictly Come Dancing this weekend, with YouTuber George currently leading as the bookmakers' favourite. Yet the competition doesn't always go according to plan, with the BBC ballroom series having witnessed numerous surprising outcomes throughout its history. The 2009 series delivered one of the programme's most unexpected moments when Chris Hollins defied the odds to triumph over favourite Ricky Whittle. The victory surprised many, with the presenter later admitting he felt somewhat guilty about his win too. Hollyoaks actor Ricky, partnered with professional dancer Natalie Lowe, had regularly out-danced Chris, who performed alongside Ola Jordan. Nevertheless, Chris garnered substantial support from the viewing public and whilst he wasn't receiving the highest judges' scores, he consistently progressed through public votes, never facing elimination in the dance-off. Ultimately, he claimed victory, reports Wales Online. Prior to the finale, Chris acknowledged feeling a little guilty about reaching the last stage instead of Ali Bastian and Brian Fortuna, who had achieved better scor", 'Strictly Come Dancing fans have same complaint as La Voix withdraws from show \'Pointless!\' Strictly Come Dancing fans have been left with the same question after it was announced La Voix has withdrawn from the competition. During Saturday (November 22) night\'s show, the remaining contestants took to the dance floor at the iconic Blackpool Tower Ballroom. Before things kicked off for Blackpool Week, presenter Tess Daly informed viewers that the drag queen – real name Chris Dennis – would not be returning to the competition after sustaining a foot injury. She said: "We can confirm she has very sadly had to withdraw from the competition," before revealing there would be no elimination this weekend. But this has left fans wondering why Sunday night\'s show is still airing now that there is no dance-off. Taking to social media with their queries, one wrote: "So what’s the point of Sunday’s show when everything can be done tonight #Strictly?" The 62-year-old actress had triggered concerns about a potential exit after her daughter disclosed a rib injury on social media, stating that her mother had endured the discomfort to participate in Saturday\'s programme. The Doctor Who legend, who has been dazzling audiences on the dance floor alongside partner Johannes Radebe, was swift to comfort her supporters that injuries were simply "part and parcel" of the Strictly experience, and she had absolutely no plans to retire her dancing shoes. Fleur East, host of Strictly spin-off It Takes Two, has now verified that Alex will return to the competition following last week\'s Blackpool special. Name: 10_immigration_britain_british government_diversity
Representative_Docs: ['‘UK public have tougher stance on immigration and diversity than people in US’ People in the UK are less likely than their US counterparts to feel diversity strengthens society and more likely to have tougher attitudes on immigration, according to research. The findings are said to show that a common narrative about Britain being more socially liberal than America does not ring true across all issues. The National Centre for Social Research also found Reform UK voters in Britain were more likely than supporters of President Donald Trump in the US to say immigrants living in their country without permission should not be allowed to stay. While seven in 10 supporters of Mr Trump felt this way, the figure was almost eight in 10 (79%) among Reform followers. The researchers said they wanted to “better understand how public attitudes in the UK compare with those in the US, and the extent of polarisation in the two countries”. Their survey found 42% of people in the UK think there should be legal efforts to deport immigrants living here without permission, compared with just a third (33%) in the US. While almost two-thirds (64%) of respondents in the US felt diversity strengthens society, only around half (49%) of the UK public agreed. Name: 12_florence_meeting_italy_economy
Representative_Docs: ['Florence Press Conference Confirms ECB Remains Comfortable in Its Good Place Even a change of scenery has done nothing to change the European Central Bank’s current wait-and-see stance. Florence, where the ECB met today for its only annual meeting outside of Frankfurt, is apparently also this ’good place’, the central bank’s well-known pseudonym for comfortably keeping interest rates on hold. ECB President Christine Lagarde even called it a ‘magnificent’ place – Florence, that is, not rates. The comments at the press conference confirmed once again that the ECB has no intention to leave its ‘good place’ any time soon. We had feared a rather uneventful press conference. What should we say? We hate to be right… Anyhow, here are a few interesting remarks made by Lagarde: On the ECB’s risk assessment, the introductory statement had the most remarkable change compared with September. Last month, the ECB had still talked about risks to the growth outlook that had become “more balanced”; today, it refrained from qualifying any balance of risk. Instead, the central bank had a somewhat more extensive list of upside and downside risks to the growth outlook. Lagarde even said that some of the downside risks had abated. Name: 14_surgical_surgeries_memorial hospital_patients
Representative_Docs: ['VIDEO: $5.5M robotic surgery system arrives at Surrey Memorial Hospital Published 12:10 pm Tuesday, January 27, 2026 By Anna Burns The da Vinci Xi System has been unpacked and calibrated and is being held in a temporary location until its permanent location is finished. Fraser Health said that it expects it to “go-live” later in the spring. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Health care workers pose in front of the da Vinci Xi System. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Surrey Memorial Hospital is one step closer to providing robotic-assisted surgery with the arrival of the da Vinci Xi Robotic Surgery system. It is expected to be used in surgeries in the spring. “With the aid of the da Vinci Xi system, we can expand minimally invasive thoracic and foregut surgery, and we can do operations with greater precision and accuracy,” says Dr Ahmad Ashrafi, regional division head and chief of thoracic surgery for Fraser Health. “Patients may experience quicker recovery times, and we can carry out more complex procedures right here in Surrey, using minimally invasive techniques.
